channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Tinubu Appoints Three Oyo Indigenes to Surveyors Council
item.description: President Bola Tinubu has approved the appointment of three Oyo State indigenes to the Surveyors Council of Nigeria. The new appointees include Moshood Akinwande as President and Olusoji Adeoye, Olari...

item.title: Council of Europe Urges FIFA to Strengthen World Cup Integrity Amid Concerns Over Political Influence and Betting Fraud
item.description: The Council of Europe has raised serious concerns about the integrity of the 2026 FIFA World Cup, which is co-hosted by the United States. Secretary General Alain Berset highlighted issues such as pol...

item.title: Trump's role in hosting World Cup amid political and logistical challenges
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump is set to present the World Cup trophy to the winner of Sunday’s final between Argentina and Spain, but his administration faces scrutiny over its handling of the tournamen...

item.title: Banyana Banyana goalkeeper dilemma: Dlamini and Swart unite ahead of Wafcon
item.description: Andile Dlamini, the first-choice goalkeeper for Banyana Banyana's historic 2022 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) victory, has insisted she and fellow keeper Kaylin Swart are best friends. Despit...

item.title: Phoenix Overcomes Ginebra's Late Rally in PBA Game
item.description: The Philippine Basketball Association (PBA) game between the Phoenix Fuel Masters and the Barangay Ginebra San Miguel saw a thrilling showdown, with Phoenix securing a hard-fought victory. Despite Gin...

item.title: Energy Secretary Chris Wright Concedes Biden Won 2020 Election, Sparks Trump's Anger
item.description: Energy Secretary Chris Wright made a rare on-air concession during an interview with ABC News' Jonathan Karl, admitting that President Donald Trump lost the 2020 election. Wright's admission, stating ...

item.title: CRDB Bank invests in youth development through Imbeju Football Academy
item.description: CRDB Bank has launched an initiative to support young football talents from the Ndondo Cup through its Imbeju Football Academy. The program aims to provide structured training, mentorship, and resourc...

item.title: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand to Visit Pakistan for Bilateral Talks on Trade and Cooperation
item.description: Canadian Foreign Minister Anita Anand is set to visit Pakistan on Monday, marking the first such visit in 20 years. The Foreign Office confirmed the trip, stating it will be Anand’s first to Pakistan ...

item.title: Residents Oppose Data Centre Plans in Historic East London Area
item.description: A proposed data centre development on Brick Lane, a culturally significant area of east London, has sparked intense community opposition. The project, which would involve constructing a 29-metre-tall ...

item.title: Former Blue Jays Pitcher Duane Ward Dies at 62
item.description: Duane Ward, a former Toronto Blue Jays relief pitcher and two-time World Series champion, has passed away at the age of 62. The team confirmed his death was due to natural causes in Toronto, where he ...

item.title: Senators clash over flood control investigations and political accountability
item.description: Senator Alan Peter Cayetano has urged the Senate Blue Ribbon Committee (BRC) to expand its investigation into alleged anomalous flood control projects beyond Taguig City, challenging Senator Panfilo L...

item.title: Targeted Killing of 30-Year-Old Man in Plateau Community
item.description: Gunmen killed a 30-year-old man, Khalid Wahid, in his home in Dong community, Jos North, Plateau State. The attack occurred around 1am on Sunday when the attackers stormed his residence, dragged him o...

item.title: Idac oversight office addresses complaints amid scrutiny of Andrea Johnson
item.description: The Investigative Directorate Against Corruption (Idac) oversight office has confirmed receiving multiple complaints, including high-profile cases, but has not disclosed specific details about allegat...

item.title: CNN Criticizes Trump's Push for Controversial SAVE America Act, Sparking GOP Division
item.description: The article discusses how CNN host Manu Raju mocked President Donald Trump's insistence on passing the SAVE America Act, a bill targeting non-citizen voting—a practice experts say does not exist. Raju...

item.title: U.S. Justice Department's Civil Rights Division Faces Criticism Over Policy Shifts and Investigation Changes
item.description: The U.S. Department of Justice’s civil rights division has come under scrutiny for its evolving approach to investigations, particularly under the Trump administration. Recent reports highlight a shif...

item.title: Arusha man's life sentence overturned after court finds rape trial flawed
item.description: The Court of Appeal in Tanzania has overturned the life imprisonment sentence of Baraka Makubi, a retail shop owner in Monduli District, after finding the rape trial flawed. The appellate panel, consi...

item.title: Hawthorn Hawks thrash lowly Richmond to climb AFL ladder
item.description: In a dominant display, Hawthorn Hawks crushed struggling Richmond by 70 points at the MCG, propelling them back into the top four of the AFL ladder. Uncontracted forward Mitch Lewis starred with five ...

item.title: TMC Leader Urges PM Modi to Hold All-Party Meeting on Delimitation and FCRA Bill Reforms
item.description: Rajya Sabha leader Derek O'Brien has written to Prime Minister Narendra Modi, urging him to convene an all-party meeting to discuss the government's proposed delimitation legislation and amendments to...

item.title: The Legacy of the 1976 Montreal Olympics Boycott and Its Impact on African Athletes
item.description: The 1976 Montreal Olympics boycott by African nations against New Zealand's participation due to apartheid policies marked a pivotal moment in sports history. The article explores how athletes like Gl...

item.title: Nine killed in two road accidents in Limpopo's Sekhukhune district
item.description: Two fatal road accidents occurred in Limpopo’s Sekhukhune district on Saturday, claiming nine lives and injuring twelve others. The first crash involved a Volkswagen Polo, minibus, and truck on the R5...
